headline: SpaceX (SPCX) now down 31pct from its high after launching a surprise 20 billion dollar bond sale - your holding
detail: What happened: SPCX, one of your holdings, kept sliding and is now about 31 percent below last weeks high (it IPOd at 135 on June 12, peaked near 226 on June 16). New trigger today: a surprise plan to borrow 20 billion dollars in its first-ever bond sale, even though the company says it holds about 100 billion in cash - investors are asking why it needs to borrow so soon after its huge IPO, and its credit ratings came in just okay (BBB, a few notches above junk). Two extra pressures: options on SPCX started trading June 17, which finally lets short sellers bet against it, and worries about losses at Musks xAI. This sits inside a broader second-day tech and chip selloff (Nasdaq about -1.3pct, S&P about -0.9pct today). Decision-support, not advice: SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with little history, so swings this big are normal but real - know your stop and your position size and do not add margin chasing it. The bond news is about financing, not about the rocket business itself changing.

headline: Tech selloff confirmed at the open - chips slammed and even gold is falling (a sell-everything move)
detail: What is happening: the overnight AI/chip selloff carried into US trading. Nvidia is down about 6 percent, Nasdaq deeply red, and South Koreas market closed down nearly 10 percent (its worst drop in months) as investors dump crowded chip bets. New and important: this is no longer just a tech rotation - gold is also down about 1.3 percent and silver about 4.5 percent. When safe-havens fall alongside stocks it usually means big players are raising cash and unwinding leverage (a deleveraging or margin-call type move), which makes swings sharper than a normal dip. Impact on you: your book sits right in the firing line - AVGO, SMH, QQQ, SPY, PLTR, SNOW, CRWD - so today is red. SpaceX (SPCX) is wavering near 147 after Mondays drop; wild swings are normal for a brand-new IPO. Also new: Oracle fell after disclosing it cut about 21,000 jobs over the past year (not a holding of yours). Decision-support, not advice: the key risk today is leverage - know where your stops are and avoid adding margin into a falling, sell-everything tape; do not panic-sell into the volatility.

headline: Global tech selloff hits AI and chip stocks - Nasdaq futures down ~2.4% before Tuesday open
detail: What happened: a broad sell-off in tech and AI names spread worldwide overnight. South Koreas market closed down 10% (Samsung and SK Hynix off more than 12%), Europe tech is lower, and US futures point sharply down (Nasdaq -2.4%, S&P -1.3%, Dow -0.6%) before Tuesdays open. Why: investors are unwinding crowded AI and chip bets on worries about stretched valuations, a possible Fed rate hike under new chair Warsh, and a report that SK Hynix may make fewer AI memory chips - read as softer AI demand. Impact on your portfolio: this hits you directly - AVGO, SMH, QQQ, SPY, PLTR, SNOW and CRWD are all in the line of fire, so expect red at the open. Your SpaceX (SPCX) also fell 16% Monday and is now about 23% below last weeks high - wild swings are normal for a brand-new IPO with little history. Decision-support, not advice: do not panic-sell into the open; know where your stops are and avoid adding margin into a falling, hawkish-Fed tape.

headline: Chips and tech led a broad rally - Nasdaq +1.9%, S&P +1.1%, Russell +2.1%
detail: What happened: a strong, broad risk-on day led by semiconductors and growth tech. Impact on the market: confirms buyers stepped back in after the early-June chip selloff and the Fed scare. Impact on your portfolio: directly positive - your book is concentrated in exactly these names (AVGO, SMH, QQQ, SPY, PLTR, SNOW, CRWD), so you had a good day. Decision-support, not advice; do not chase strength - let winners run and keep your stops.

headline: U.S. chipmaking optimism: Intel jumped ~9% on a reported Intel-Apple chip partnership
detail: What happened: renewed enthusiasm for U.S.-based chip production lifted the whole semiconductor group. Impact on the market: bullish for the AI/chip trade that has been leading 2026. Impact on your portfolio: a tailwind for AVGO and SMH (your chip exposure). Note Broadcom (AVGO, your largest holding) has been volatile after a light AI guidance earlier this month - a sector tailwind helps it stabilize, but it remains your weakest position (~-13%); watch it on Monday.

headline: Geopolitics cooled: U.S. ended the Iran blockade and a peace deal is in place; oil eased
detail: What happened: the Middle East conflict de-escalated, pulling oil back and removing a major worry. Impact on the market: risk-on - good for stocks broadly, especially rate-sensitive tech. Impact on your portfolio: supportive for your tech/chip-heavy book; lower oil also eases inflation pressure (a small plus given the Fed is leaning hawkish). You own no energy, so the oil pullback does not hurt you.

headline: Market shook off the Fed: stocks rebounded after Warsh signaled a possible 2026 hike
detail: What happened: Tuesday the Fed (new chair Kevin Warsh) held rates but signaled a possible HIKE this year and took cuts off the table, causing a 6/17 dip - then the market recovered Wednesday/Thursday. Impact on the market: higher-for-longer is a mild headwind, but buyers looked through it. Impact on your portfolio: keep an eye on your pricier growth names (PLTR, SNOW, CRWD) and QQQ, which are the most rate-sensitive; nothing urgent, just do not over-extend on margin into a hawkish Fed.
